evidence_refs.quote: We investigate whether an online adaptive landmark reweighting policy can preserve answer-critical key-value (KV) cache state under tighter memory budgets than static landmark pinning. Rather than pinning block-level landmarks and high-salience spans once at prefill, the adaptive policy re-scores spans after each generated paragraph, boosting currently-needed spans and their block neighbors, decaying cold spans, and applying hysteresis-gated promote/quantize/evict/restore decisions.
